title: Predict Paint Order across Nested Stacking Contexts
summary: Build and verify css stacking contexts in sticky filter and card badge layer conflict through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css stacking contexts from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css stacking contexts without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.title: 05 · Read the sticky filter and card badge layer conflict case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 5: Which claim preserves css stacking contexts when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: sticky filter and card badge layer conflict.
steps.why: Predict Paint Order across Nested Stacking Contexts makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 5 in sticky filter and card badge layer conflict.

content.text: Stacking contexts are created by positioned elements with z-index and by properties such as opacity, transforms, filters, isolation, and containment. Descendant z-index values compete only inside their context. Paint order compares sibling contexts first, then their internal layers. Top-layer UI such as dialogs follows a separate mechanism. Apply it to sticky filter and card badge layer conflict.
